career_angle: Post-training data work (RLHF, evals, tool-use training) is genuinely booming -- if you have domain expertise in coding, APIs, or ML evaluation, companies like Deccan, Scale AI, and Surge are hiring aggressively. Don't join for the equity story; join for the skills in AI evaluation pipelines that transfer everywhere.

factors.rationale: TechCrunch is tier-1 tech press; Ben's Bites is a credible aggregator. Source is solid.
Specific investors and round size are verifiable, but zero revenue figures, workforce numbers, or customer engagement metrics. All claims are company-sourced.
Funding round verifiable via Crunchbase/PitchBook. Customer relationships with DeepMind and Snowflake are name-drops with no scope or contract details.
Classic fundraise announcement piece. Company has every incentive to overstate traction. No independent analyst or customer quotes to counterbalance.
AI post-training and RLHF outsourcing is a validated, growing market. Scale AI, Surge AI, Labelbox all prove the category. Not a speculative bet.
Names products (Helix) and use cases (coding, APIs, world models) but everything stays at brochure-level. No benchmarks, no before/after, no customer testimonials.
Founded October 2024 -- 17 months old. No prior exits, no public track record. Too early to evaluate execution ability beyond raising money.
